question_text: Module 4 builds on the earlier modules. According to the introduction, the central question that Module 4 sets out to answer is:
answer_options: Who am I now that I am in Christ?
Who is God and what is His character?
How does Christ save and grant righteousness?
What is God's plan for humanity and the fall of man?
correct_answer: Who am I now that I am in Christ?

question_text: The lesson quotes 2 Corinthians 5:17. According to that verse as given in the text, if any man is in Christ:
answer_options: he is a new creature; old things are passed away and all things are become new
he is forgiven of his sins only and nothing else changes
he must work to become accepted by God
he remains an old man who is simply trying to improve
correct_answer: he is a new creature; old things are passed away and all things are become new

question_text: The lesson teaches that man has three dimensions based on 1 Thessalonians 5:23. According to the text, the soul is:
answer_options: the mind, will, and emotions
the inner man
the outward man
the part made alive first at salvation

question_text: The lesson distinguishes between two voices that can address a believer over sin. According to the text, what is the difference?
answer_options: The devil guilts, while the Holy Spirit convicts
The devil convicts, while the Holy Spirit guilts
Both the devil and the Holy Spirit guilt the believer
Neither guilt nor conviction has any effect on the believer
correct_answer: The devil guilts, while the Holy Spirit convicts

question_text: The text describes the condition of man before salvation, citing Ephesians 2:1. According to the lesson, spiritual death means:
answer_options: separation from the life of God, being cut off from God while possibly active outwardly
the complete end of a person's existence
a temporary loss of physical strength and health
a state in which a person merely needs better advice and discipline
correct_answer: separation from the life of God, being cut off from God while possibly active outwardly

question_text: The lesson explains where and when the new birth takes place in a person. According to the text, salvation is:
answer_options: instant in the spirit, then worked out progressively in the soul, and finally completed even in the body
instant in the body first, then later worked out in the spirit
a gradual change that begins in the soul and never reaches the spirit
completed fully and visibly in every area on the day of conversion
correct_answer: instant in the spirit, then worked out progressively in the soul, and finally completed even in the body

question_text: The lesson addresses a believer who still struggles with old thought patterns, emotional wounds, or learned habits. According to the text, what does this struggle prove?
answer_options: that sanctification is now needed, not that the new birth is false
that the new birth never actually happened
that the believer's spirit was not made new
that the believer has lost his sonship and must be saved again
correct_answer: that sanctification is now needed, not that the new birth is false

question_text: The lesson teaches on the believer's restored authority, referencing passages such as Mark 16:17–18. According to the text, this authority is:
answer_options: not self-generated, but exercised in Christ's name, under His lordship, and by faith in what He accomplished
self-generated power that the believer produces by his own strength
a fight to win Christ's victory that has not yet been achieved
lawful dominion that the devil still holds over the believer
correct_answer: not self-generated, but exercised in Christ's name, under His lordship, and by faith in what He accomplished

question_text: The lesson explains what happens when a Christian sins. According to the text, when a Christian sins, that sin:
answer_options: does not become his identity but contradicts it, disrupting fellowship without erasing sonship
becomes his new and deepest identity
permanently erases his sonship and standing before God
may be excused freely because identity in Christ is settled
correct_answer: does not become his identity but contradicts it, disrupting fellowship without erasing sonship

question_text: The lesson repeatedly contrasts identity and growth, summarizing the Christian life. According to the text, which statement best captures its core teaching?
answer_options: Identity in Christ is already settled, and the Christian life is learning to live outwardly from what God already did inwardly
Identity in Christ is gradually earned as a believer's conduct improves over time
Growth in the soul must be complete before a believer's identity can change
A believer's identity is built from his feelings, past, tribe, wounds, and failures
correct_answer: Identity in Christ is already settled, and the Christian life is learning to live outwardly from what God already did inwardly
